nodes.description: The frontend is a single large Next.js page that acts as the operating surface for the whole product. It manages repo selection, open tabs, console state, build and plan actions, note editing, and project status in one client-side workspace instead of separate pages. The evidence is the `Home` component orchestrating status refresh, streaming runs, canvas refresh, conversation loading, and git actions.
nodes.title: Workspace Shell

nodes.description: The primary UI surface is a visual note canvas where architecture concepts are created, edited, linked, explored, and repositioned. It shows only selected or exploration-related edges, persists viewport per repo, and supports contextual actions like open, rename, activate, and delete. The implementation is grounded in React Flow via `CanvasBoard` and the note node renderer.
nodes.title: Architecture Canvas

nodes.description: Canvas notes are treated as durable workspace metadata for a specific repository, not as transient UI state. The backend stores the active canvas pointer centrally and mirrors the actual document into `<repo>/.konceptura/canvas.json`, while enforcing tag normalization, unique edge semantics, and automatic cleanup when notes are deleted. This is implemented by `CanvasStore` and `CanvasService`.
nodes.title: Canvas Note Store

nodes.description: Canvas edits can be proposed before they are applied, so note changes are reviewable rather than immediate. The backend computes impacted notes from shared files, linked symbols, and existing note edges, asks Codex for a structured change set, then applies only accepted changes while honoring dependencies between creates, updates, deletes, and edges. This gives the canvas its controlled editing policy.
nodes.title: Canvas Change Review

nodes.description: Konceptura keeps track of the active repository, recent repositories, visible file counts, and whether the workspace already has notes. This is the product boundary that turns an arbitrary local folder into the current project context, and it also creates `.konceptura/.gitignore` so workspace metadata stays isolated from normal source files. The behavior is centered in `ProjectService`.
nodes.title: Project Registry

nodes.description: Each repository gets its own saved conversation history so build, plan, and ask workflows can accumulate local context over time. Conversations are stored under `.konceptura/conversations.json`, listed in the project tree, and updated as the user interacts with the console. The code shows this as repository-scoped records rather than a global chat log.
nodes.title: Conversation Memory

nodes.description: The backend can index the active repository into the code graph by invoking the local `code-graph-rag` binary and tracking job state in `backend/data/state.json`. It supports dry-run previews, clean reindexing, and updates the active repo only when indexing succeeds. This makes repository analysis an explicit workflow rather than an implicit background task.
nodes.title: Graph Indexing

nodes.description: Once indexed, the repository is exposed as searchable symbols, relationship queries, and a hierarchical structure tree over Memgraph. `GraphService` wraps Neo4j-compatible queries, serializes graph objects into JSON, ranks symbol search results, and reconstructs a project tree from containment and definition edges. This is the main analysis boundary between the app and Memgraph.
nodes.title: Graph Query and Structure

nodes.description: Prompt-driven impact analysis turns free-text change intent into probable symbols, related dependencies, and candidate files. The service uses token matching over graph nodes, then expands through `CALLS` and `IMPORTS` edges to summarize likely implementation touchpoints. This is the main business logic that injects semantic code awareness into editing workflows.
nodes.title: Impact Analysis

nodes.description: Codex is used as a local code assistant for direct changes, plans, project questions, architecture note generation, and exploration suggestions. The backend snapshots the repo, builds prompts from semantic and graph context, executes the Codex CLI, parses JSONL events, and derives changed-file diffs and summaries. This service is the boundary that turns the workspace into an AI-assisted editor.
nodes.title: Codex Orchestrator

nodes.description: The main execution path can resolve a user request as ask, plan, or build and stream phased progress plus assistant chunks back to the UI. In build mode it chains code edits with architecture-note generation and returns both file changes and the updated canvas document, so the workspace stays synchronized with implementation work. This is the product workflow that ties the console to both code and notes.
nodes.title: Streaming Run Pipeline

nodes.description: The project surface includes commit and push actions with branch status, ahead/behind counts, changed files, and AI-suggested commit messages. Git operations are intentionally separate from Codex editing so the user can inspect and ship changes explicitly from the workspace. The implementation uses `ProjectService` for git commands and `CodexService` only for commit-message suggestion.
nodes.title: Git Delivery Controls

nodes.description: The app is designed for local self-hosting and reads its operating contract from `konceptura.toml`, including repo defaults, Memgraph connection, binary paths, CORS origins, and backend data files. `Settings` resolves relative paths against the workspace root and can discover the Codex binary automatically, which keeps deployment assumptions local and explicit. The README and dev scripts show the intended local stack: Memgraph, Python API, and Next.js frontend.
nodes.title: Runtime Configuration
